adjectives.meaning: Large in size, not small
adjectives.example: My dad has a big blue car that fits our whole family of five people.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Big means large. An elephant is big. A mouse is not big.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is big. (The house is big.)

adjectives.meaning: Little in size, not big
adjectives.example: I found a small ladybug crawling on a green leaf in our garden.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Small means little, like an ant or a button.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is small. (The bug is small.)

adjectives.meaning: Feeling good, joyful, and pleased
adjectives.example: I feel happy when I play with my friends at the park on sunny days.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Happy is when you feel good and you smile a lot.
adjectives.sentence_builder: I am happy when ______. (I am happy when I play.)

adjectives.meaning: Feeling unhappy, down, or upset
adjectives.example: I feel sad when my favorite toy breaks and I cannot fix it.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Sad is when you feel like crying or your mouth makes a frown.
adjectives.sentence_builder: I feel sad when ______. (I feel sad when I lose my toy.)

adjectives.meaning: Having a high temperature, very warm
adjectives.example: The soup is very hot, so I blow on it carefully before I take a bite.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Hot means very warm, like the sun, a fire, or soup from a pot.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is hot. (The stove is hot.)

adjectives.meaning: Having a low temperature, not warm
adjectives.example: The ice cream is cold and delicious on a hot summer day at the beach.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Cold means not warm, like ice, snow, or a cold drink.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is cold. (The ice cube is cold.)

adjectives.meaning: Moving or able to move quickly
adjectives.example: The cheetah is very fast and can run faster than a car on a highway.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Fast means moving quickly, like a race car or a rocket.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is fast. (The train is fast.)

adjectives.meaning: Moving at a low speed, not fast
adjectives.example: The turtle is very slow but very cute, and he always wins in the story!
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Slow means not fast, like a snail or a turtle.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is slow. (The snail is slow.)

adjectives.meaning: Pleasant to touch, squishy, not hard or rough
adjectives.example: My warm blanket is soft and keeps me cozy at night when I sleep.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Soft means nice and squishy to touch, like a pillow or a cat's fur.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is soft. (The teddy bear is soft.)

adjectives.meaning: Firm and solid, difficult to press or break
adjectives.example: The rock is so hard that I cannot break it, even if I try very hard.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Hard means not soft, like a table, a rock, or a floor.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is hard. (The floor is hard.)

adjectives.meaning: Making a lot of noise, easy to hear
adjectives.example: The fire truck makes a loud siren that wakes me up in the morning.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Loud means noisy, like a drum, a fire alarm, or a cheering crowd.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is loud. (The bell is loud.)

adjectives.meaning: Making very little noise, silent or almost silent
adjectives.example: The library is very quiet so that people can read their books peacefully.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Quiet means not noisy, like a whisper or a sleeping baby.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is quiet. (The mouse is quiet.)

adjectives.meaning: Giving out a lot of light, shining strongly
adjectives.example: The flashlight is very bright in the dark room and helps me see.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Bright means shining a lot of light, like the sun or a light bulb.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is bright. (The star is bright.)

adjectives.meaning: With little or no light, hard to see
adjectives.example: It is very dark outside when I go to bed, so I use a night light.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Dark means no light, like night time or a closet with the door closed.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is dark. (The cave is dark.)

adjectives.meaning: Not old, recently made, bought, or found
adjectives.example: I have new shoes that light up with bright colors when I take a step.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: New means fresh and never used before, like a toy still in the box.
adjectives.sentence_builder: I have a new ______. (I have a new backpack.)

adjectives.meaning: Having existed for a long time, not new
adjectives.example: My grandma has an old photograph from when she was a little girl.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Old means something that has been here a long time, like a grandparent or an antique.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is old. (The tree is old.)

adjectives.meaning: Not dirty, free from dirt or stains
adjectives.example: I put on clean clothes after I take a bath with soap and water.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Clean means not dirty. Your hands are clean after you wash them.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is clean. (The plate is clean.)

adjectives.meaning: Not clean, covered with dirt or stains
adjectives.example: My hands are dirty after playing in the mud and making mud pies.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Dirty means messy or not clean, like clothes after playing outside.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is dirty. (The shirt is dirty.)

adjectives.meaning: Tasting like sugar, honey, or candy
adjectives.example: Fresh strawberries are sweet and yummy, especially with whipped cream.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Sweet means tasting like candy, cookies, or cake.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is sweet. (The apple is sweet.)

adjectives.meaning: Having a sharp, tangy taste that makes your mouth pucker
adjectives.example: Lemons are very sour and make my face scrunch up when I bite them.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Sour means a sharp taste, like a lemon, lime, or sour candy.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is sour. (The pickle is sour.)

adjectives.meaning: Having a greater than average height
adjectives.example: My big brother is tall and can reach things on the top shelf for me.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Tall means high up, like a giraffe or a skyscraper.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is tall. (The building is tall.)

adjectives.meaning: Having a small height, not tall
adjectives.example: I am short compared to my dad, but I will grow taller someday.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Short means not tall, like a puppy or a pencil.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is short. (The grass is short.)

adjectives.meaning: Weighing a lot, difficult to lift
adjectives.example: The box of books is heavy, so I need help from my dad to carry it.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Heavy means something weighs a lot and is hard to pick up.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is heavy. (The rock is heavy.)

adjectives.meaning: Weighing very little, easy to lift
adjectives.example: A feather is light and floats gently down to the ground.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Light means something is easy to lift, like a piece of paper.
adjectives.sentence_builder: The ______ is light. (The balloon is light.)
